notes: Assertion 'spawns subagent' fails in both configurations for evals 1 and 4 because the Agent tool is not available inside eval executor subagents. This is an eval harness constraint, not a skill defect.
Eval 2 is the primary discriminating eval (+0.40 delta): spec mode detection and structured reviewer delegation differentiate with_skill from without_skill.
Eval 4 (diff mode) is the secondary discriminating eval (+0.20 delta): explicit diff mode declaration differentiates, subagent assertion fails in both due to harness constraint.
Eval 3 (argument-conflict-error) is non-discriminating: both configurations correctly detect the mutually exclusive target conflict and output the error without running a review.
Eval 1 is zero-delta (0.80/0.80) due to harness constraint masking the subagent-spawning differentiator.

Evals 5-10 test Phase II multi-LLM routing. Eval 6 (copilot-empty-findings) is non-discriminating: empty findings → 'No issues found.' is natural default behavior. Evals 5, 7, 8, 9, 10 all discriminate.
Evals 5-10 use simulated transcripts (fixture responses embedded in prompts); time_seconds and tokens are null for these runs.

Evals 11-14 added in v1.1 review pass. Eval 13 (focus-option) discriminates (+0.67 delta): the skill-defined focus line format and apply prompt both fail without_skill. Evals 11, 12, 14 are non-discriminating: empty-staged warning, PR metadata inclusion (when fixture data is explicit in the prompt), and skip handling are natural baseline behaviors.
Evals 11-14 have real time and token measurements (subagent executor ran the full workflow, not pure transcript simulation).
Evals 15-20 added in v1.2 for triage layer and post-apply re-scan. All use simulated fixtures; time_seconds and tokens are null.

Evals 15 (triage-skips-false-positive) and 16 (triage-all-skipped) and 19 (rescan-offered-after-apply) are discriminating. Evals 17, 18, 20 are non-discriminating: triage-not-on-claude-path and rescan-not-offered-after-skip are natural baseline behaviors; triage-user-includes-skipped passes because 'S1' literal selection is intuitive.

Evals 2, 21, 22, 23 added/re-run in v1.4. Eval 2 (consistency-mode-plan-tasks-mismatch) re-run with v1.3-compatible assertions — discriminating (+0.40 delta). Eval 21 (both-staged-and-unstaged-prompt) discriminates (+0.67 delta): disambiguation prompt is entirely skill-defined. Evals 22 (unstaged-only-auto-review) and 23 (staged-explicit-bypasses-detection) are non-discriminating: these behaviors are intuitive enough that the baseline handles them correctly. Adding evals 22 and 23 diluted the headline delta from +0.30 to +0.29.

Summary-table delta values are computed from unrounded means (with_skill=0.97391, without_skill=0.67957), so they may differ slightly from subtracting the displayed rounded means. Time/token statistics are computed only over the 7 primary runs with non-null measurements (evals 1, 3, 4, 11, 12, 13, 14); the remaining 16 evals use simulated transcripts.
